evals.prompt: I need global 2m temperature data for January 1, 2024 at 00Z. What data sources can provide this in Earth2Studio? Pick the fastest option and run the fetch script.
evals.expected_output: Presents multiple candidate analysis data sources (e.g. GFS, ARCO, WB2ERA5) that support t2m globally, explains tradeoffs, selects one, then generates and runs the script.
assertions: Presents at least two candidate data sources that support t2m
Explains a tradeoff between the options (speed, coverage, auth, resolution)
Selects one source and justifies the choice
Script executes successfully and produces an xarray DataArray
Execution completes in under 3 minutes

evals.prompt: Fetch 500 hPa geopotential height from ERA5 for 2020-06-15 12Z. I don't have a CDS API key so use a source that doesn't need authentication. Write and run the script.
evals.expected_output: Selects ARCO or WB2ERA5 (no auth required) instead of CDS. Generates a fetch script for z500 and runs it to produce an xarray DataArray.
assertions: Selects a no-auth ERA5 source (ARCO or WB2ERA5, not CDS)
Uses the correct variable name z500 or equivalent
Calls the source with correct analysis signature: ds(time, variable)
Script executes successfully and produces an xarray DataArray
Execution completes in under 3 minutes

evals.prompt: I want high-resolution surface wind data over North America for 2024-03-10 at 18Z. What are my options? Choose one and run the script.
evals.expected_output: Identifies sources with NA coverage (e.g. HRRR, GFS) and explains resolution differences. Selects one (likely HRRR for high-res NA) and runs the fetch script for u10m or v10m.
assertions: Presents at least two candidate data sources with North America coverage
Discusses resolution or regional coverage as a differentiator
Selects one source and generates a working fetch script
Script executes successfully and produces an xarray DataArray
Execution completes in under 3 minutes

evals.prompt: I need a 48-hour forecast of mean sea level pressure initialized on 2024-01-15 00Z. What forecast sources are available? Select one and run the fetch.
evals.expected_output: Presents forecast source options (e.g. GFS_FX, IFS_FX, AIFS_FX), explains tradeoffs, selects one, and runs the script with a 48h lead time.
assertions: Presents at least two candidate forecast data sources
Explains a tradeoff between the options (resolution, ensemble, availability)
Selects one source and uses the forecast signature: ds(time, lead_time, variable)
Script executes successfully and produces an xarray DataArray
Execution completes in under 3 minutes

evals.prompt: Fetch IFS ensemble forecast for t2m initialized 2024-02-01 00Z at lead times of 24h and 72h. Generate and run the script.
evals.expected_output: Generates a script using IFS_ENS_FX forecast source with multiple lead times. Runs it and produces a DataArray with ensemble and lead_time dimensions.
assertions: Uses IFS ensemble forecast source class (IFS_ENS_FX or equivalent)
Calls the source with forecast signature including multiple lead times
Specifies lead_time as array with 24h and 72h
Script executes successfully and produces an xarray DataArray
Execution completes in under 3 minutes

evals.prompt: I want ensemble precipitation forecasts 5 days out from 2024-04-01 00Z. What ensemble forecast sources in Earth2Studio support total precipitation? Pick one and run it.
evals.expected_output: Identifies ensemble forecast sources that support tp (e.g. GEFS_FX, IFS_ENS_FX). Explains differences, selects one, and runs the fetch with 120h lead time.
assertions: Presents at least two ensemble forecast sources that support precipitation
Explains a tradeoff between the ensemble options
Selects one source and uses the forecast signature with 120h lead time
Script executes successfully and produces an xarray DataArray
Execution completes in under 3 minutes

evals.prompt: I want surface weather station temperature observations. What dataframe data sources are available in Earth2Studio for ground station data? Pick one and fetch some data.
evals.expected_output: Presents available observational dataframe sources for surface station data, selects one, and runs a fetch script to produce observation data.
assertions: Presents at least one dataframe data source for station observations
Explains what type of observations the source provides
Selects a source and generates a working fetch script
Script executes successfully and produces output data
Execution completes in under 3 minutes

evals.prompt: Fetch buoy ocean surface temperature observations using a dataframe source in Earth2Studio. Write the script and execute it.
evals.expected_output: Identifies and uses an ocean buoy dataframe data source. Generates a fetch script for sea surface temperature observations and runs it.
assertions: Uses a DataFrame data source class for buoy/ocean observations
Script imports from earth2studio.data
Fetches ocean or sea surface temperature data
Script executes successfully and produces output data
Execution completes in under 3 minutes

evals.prompt: I need upper-air observations from aircraft. What dataframe sources in Earth2Studio provide AMDAR or aircraft data? Select one and run a fetch for temperature.
evals.expected_output: Identifies dataframe sources for aircraft/upper-air observations, selects one, and runs a fetch script to produce observation data.
assertions: Presents at least one dataframe source for aircraft/upper-air observations
Explains what variables or levels the source provides
Selects a source and generates a working fetch script
Script executes successfully and produces output data
Execution completes in under 3 minutes
